summary: The input batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. PROTOCOL 1 (THE SPLITTER) was applied to 'በለፈለፉበዌስት', separating it into 'በለፈለፉ' and 'ዌስት', and 'ኮትቻአፈር' into 'ኮትቻ' and 'አፈር', as these were clear morphological merges. PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE) found no words that were entirely meaningless and required discarding. PROTOCOL 3 (THE CORRECTOR) was used for 'ተራራራራማ', which was corrected to 'ተራራማ' (mountainous) due to an apparent OCR repetition error. Similarly, 'በስትቀር' was corrected to 'በስተቀር' (except, apart from), recognizing a common OCR confusion between 'ስ' and 'ሰ' and the prevalence of the corrected phrase. 'ጉለሌሞ' and 'ወረሂመኑ' were identified as proper nouns based on their structure and common Amharic naming conventions. PROTOCOL 4 (THE LEMMATIZER) successfully identified the infinitive forms for all valid Amharic verbs. PROTOCOL 5 (THE TRANSLATOR) provided comprehensive English synonyms. PROTOCOL 6 (THE GRAMMARIAN) assigned a single high-level part of speech to each word. PROTOCOL 7 (THE FILTER) ensured all included words were at least two characters long. All words were successfully processed and included in the output.

summary: The batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting due to merged forms: 'መነሾዎችየታዳጊ' was split into 'መነሾዎች' and 'የታዳጊ'; 'አይኖርበትምን' into 'አይኖርበትም' and 'ምን'; 'ባላገኘውና' into 'ባላገኘው' and 'እና'; 'ላይሙሉ' into 'ላይ' and 'ሙሉ'; 'ራሳቸውንበራሳቸው' into 'ራሳቸውን' and 'በራሳቸው'; and 'ግኘትእድ' into 'ግኘት' and 'ዕድል'. Two words, 'ክርከኖሼ' and 'ማውሂባ', were deemed semantic nonsense and discarded as per PROTOCOL 2. Minor OCR corrections were applied: 'ቢኮንንም' was corrected to 'ቢሆንም' (a common OCR error for 'ሆ' vs 'ኮ'), 'በተጐለተበት' was corrected to 'በተጎለተበት' (standardizing 'ጐ' to 'ጎ'), and 'እድ' was corrected to 'ዕድል' (a common informal spelling or OCR variant). All remaining words were lemmatized, translated, and assigned a high-level part of speech. All single-character words were filtered out, though none were present after initial processing.

The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting (PROTOCOL 1) at clear morphological boundaries, such as 'ከዛጉዌ' into 'ከ' and 'ዛጉዌ', and 'በወለዱም' into 'በ', 'ወለዱ', and 'ም'.

PROTOCOL 2 (JUDGE) led to the discarding of 'ገበራል' as semantically meaningless. 'ሰነአ' from 'በሰነአ' was also discarded due to its ambiguous nature and inability to be corrected with certainty.

PROTOCOL 3 (CORRECTOR) involved scholarly guesses for minor OCR errors. 'ማህበረብ' was corrected to 'ማህበረሰብ' due to its high frequency and clear semantic intent. 'በእርግርጥ' was corrected to 'ርግጥ' (after splitting 'በ') as 'እርግርጥ' is a common misspelling of 'ርግጥ'. 'የሚያስፈልገ' was treated as a truncated form and completed to 'የሚያስፈልግ' to represent its full meaning.

PROTOCOL 4 (LEMMATIZER) was applied to identify the base dictionary form for all valid words, ensuring global morphological analysis rather than simple prefix/suffix stripping. Examples include 'ሠልሦ' to 'ሦስት', 'አለመተግበርን' to 'መተግበር', and 'የተረዳችሁበትን' to 'መረዳት'.

PROTOCOL 7 (FILTER) was strictly applied, leading to the exclusion of all single-character words, including common Amharic prepositions, conjunctions, and particles such as 'የ', 'በ', 'ከ', 'ም', and 'ና', as per the rule that 'Amharic words must be at least 2 characters long'. This significantly impacted the final dataset by removing these fundamental linguistic elements when they appeared as standalone words after splitting. For instance, from 'ከዛጉዌ', only 'ዛጉዌ' was kept, and 'ከ' was discarded. Similarly, from 'በወለዱም', only 'ወለዱ' was retained.

All remaining words were assigned comprehensive English synonyms (PROTOCOL 5) and a high-level part of speech (PROTOCOL 6) from the allowed categories (Noun, Verb, Adjective, Adverb, Pronoun, Conjunction, Interjection).

The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting due to merged conjunctions or pronouns: 'ገተርእኛም' was split into 'ገተር' and 'እኛም'; 'ስለውበትና' into 'ስለውበት' and 'ና'; 'ስትጠጋውና' into 'ስትጠጋው' and 'ና'; 'ጤናችንና' into 'ጤናችን' and 'ና'; and 'ከሚሉበትና' into 'ከሚሉበት' and 'ና'. No words were discarded as semantic nonsense. Scholarly Guesses were made for common OCR errors or transliteration variants: 'ሄልሂንኪ' was corrected to 'ሄልሲንኪ' (Helsinki), 'ገተር' was identified as 'ኳታር' (Qatar), 'ምጣቤ' was corrected to 'ምጣኔ' (measure/rate), and 'ለጀሴፊን' was corrected to 'ጆሴፊን' (Josephine). All words and their derived roots met the minimum two-character length requirement.

summary: The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. PROTOCOL 1 (THE SPLITTER) identified and separated merged words such as 'ወጠረውና' into 'ወጠረው' and 'ና', 'ነገርኳቸውና' into 'ነገርኳቸው' and 'ና', 'እርስቱንና' into 'እርስቱን' and 'ና', 'ለአውሮፓውያንም' into 'ለአውሮፓውያን' and 'ም', 'የተበደረችውአቅም' into 'የተበደረችው' and 'አቅም', 'ሲያዋክቡትና' into 'ሲያዋክቡት' and 'ና', and 'ስህተቶችአይደለም' into 'ስህተቶች' and 'አይደለም'. PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE) and PROTOCOL 3 (THE CORRECTOR) led to the discarding of 'ወጨውን' and 'ያዙርልማን'. 'ወጨውን' was deemed semantically meaningless as 'ወጨ' is not a recognized Amharic root, and no confident correction could be made. 'ያዙርልማን' was also discarded due to its ambiguous morphology and lack of a standard Amharic meaning; the 'ማን' suffix rendered it unclear and not a standard construction. All other words were successfully lemmatized, translated, and tagged according to the specified protocols.

Processed 30 input strings. Applied PROTOCOL 1 (SPLITTER) to 'ምክንያቱምየሚቀበላቸውና' and 'በፊትአውራሪነት', splitting them into two valid words each. PROTOCOL 3 (CORRECTOR) was used to fix a minor OCR error in 'የሚተዳደሩተም' to 'የሚተዳደሩትም', as the correction was certain. PROTOCOL 2 (JUDGE) and PROTOCOL 3 (CORRECTOR) led to the discarding of 'መገግሟ' and 'ደንፉ' due to ambiguous spelling and lack of certain correction. Similarly, 'እናስነብባች' and 'ሁዋለን' were discarded as they appeared to be fragments of a larger word ('እናስነብባችኋለን') and were not complete, semantically meaningful words on their own.

The batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words were split: 'ነውያው' into 'ነው' and 'ያው'; 'ከጎንድር' into 'ከ' and 'ጎንድር'; 'በወፈፌነት' into 'በ' and 'ወፈፌነት'; 'የስኮትላንድና' into 'የ', 'ስኮትላንድ', and 'ና'; 'የጥቂትና' into 'የ', 'ጥቂት', and 'ና'; 'እንደምንደርስና' into 'እንደምንደርስ' and 'ና'; 'ባሽርና' into 'ባሽር' and 'ና'; and 'ማዱግሪና' into 'ማዱግሪ' and 'ና'. Single-character words ('ከ', 'በ', 'የ', 'ና') were discarded per Protocol 7. 'ንዳባኒንጊ', 'ሲቶሌን', and 'ኅረያ' were discarded as semantic nonsense or unrecognizable Amharic forms (Protocol 2). Minor OCR corrections were applied: 'አግዚእ' was corrected to 'እግዚእ' (Lord/Master) and 'ጎንድር' to 'ጎንደር' (Gonder, a city name). All remaining valid words were lemmatized, translated, and assigned a part of speech.

The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. No merged words were identified by PROTOCOL 1. PROTOCOL 2 and 3 led to the discarding of 'አልተቋ' due to its incompleteness and ambiguity, making reliable correction impossible. PROTOCOL 7 confirmed all included words and their roots were at least two characters long. Scholarly Guesses were made for proper nouns like 'ኔቪ', 'ሩት', and 'ዋቻሞ', which were treated as such. 'ገመኔ' was lemmatized to 'ገመና' (destiny/secret) as the most probable root in a general context. 'ጥፈው' was lemmatized to 'መጻፍ' (to write) due to its higher frequency compared to 'መጥፈፍ' (to braid). 'ሚደረግ' was recognized as a common colloquial variant of 'የሚደረግ'. 'ፅጥታና' was corrected to 'ጽጥታ' (quietness) by addressing a less common 'ፅ' variant and stripping the conjunction 'ና'.

summary: The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting due to merged morphemes, such as 'ፒርስበአሜሪካ' into 'ፒርስ' and 'በአሜሪካ', 'ያለአስታማሚ' into 'ያለ' and 'አስታማሚ', and words ending with '-ና' (meaning 'and') like 'እንደሚፈቀድና', 'በዲኘሎማና', 'እንጠጋና', 'በባህራምና', and 'ካልተጣጣመና' were split into their base word and 'እና'. Minor OCR correction was applied to 'ነበሩዋችው' which was corrected to 'ነበሩዋቸው' (they had them), and 'ዲኘሎማ' to 'ዲፕሎማ' (diploma). The word 'ከስማ' was discarded as per PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE) due to its high ambiguity and potential for semantic nonsense without further context; it could mean 'from a name' or 'listen!', making a definitive root and translation a mere guess. All remaining words were lemmatized to their infinitive or base noun form, assigned a part of speech, and provided with comprehensive English synonyms. All single-character words were filtered out, though none were present after splitting and discarding.

summary: The batch of Amharic words was processed according to all 8 scholarly protocols. No words required splitting (PROTOCOL 1) as they were correctly segmented. All words were semantically valid (PROTOCOL 2) and did not require discarding. Minor corrections involved stripping implicit punctuation (e.g., 'ና' for 'and') and ensuring the base form. Each word was successfully lemmatized to its global citation form (PROTOCOL 4), translated with comprehensive synonyms (PROTOCOL 5), and assigned a high-level part of speech (PROTOCOL 6). All words met the minimum length requirement (PROTOCOL 7). Proper nouns like 'ፌይቢየንንና' and 'ኢዛቤልን' were identified and tagged as Nouns with appropriate translations.
